message.content:
i noticed in recent merge we changed the sidebar background color in @packages/ui/src/globals.css 

we need to override the sidebar background color for @apps/www/src/app/(app)/(content)/docs/_components/docs-sidebar.tsx to be the actual background color of our dark background.

update @apps/www/src/styles/globals.css

content.text: Looking at the CSS, the dark sidebar is `oklch(0.155 0 0)` while the dark background is `oklch(0.2178 0 0)`. I'll override `--sidebar` in the www globals to match the background.

content.content: The file /Users/jeevanpillay/Code/@lightfastai/lightfast/apps/www/src/styles/globals.css has been updated successfully.

lines: +  --sidebar: oklch(0.2178 0 0); /* Match background — docs sidebar should not contrast */

content.text: Done. The docs sidebar dark background now matches `--background` (`oklch(0.2178 0 0)`) instead of the darker `oklch(0.155 0 0)` from the shared UI theme.

# Oneshot Merge

You are tasked with taking the current changes, committing them, creating a PR, and merging into main — all in one flow with user approval.

## Process:

1. **Understand what changed:**
   - Review the conversation history and understand what was accomplished
   - Run `git status` to see current changes
   - Run `git diff` to understand the modifications
   - Run `git log --oneline -5` to see recent commit history and branch state

2. **Plan your commit(s):**
   - Identify which files belong together
   - Draft clear, descriptive commit messages (imperative mood, focus on why)
   - Consider whether changes should be one commit or multiple logical commits
   - Draft a PR title (under 70 chars) and summary

3. **Present the full plan to the user:**
   - List the files you plan to add for each commit
   - Show the commit message(s) you'll use
   - Show the PR title and summary
   - Confirm: merge target is `main`
   - Ask: "I plan to create [N] commit(s), open a PR, and merge to main. Shall I proceed?"

4. **Execute upon confirmation:**
   - Create a branch if not already on one (use a descriptive branch name)
   - Use `git add` with specific files (never use `-A` or `.`)
   - Create commits with your planned messages
   - Push to remote with `-u` flag
   - Create PR using `gh pr create` with title and body
   - Merge using `gh pr merge --merge --delete-branch`
   - Show the merged PR URL

## Remember:

- You have the full context of what was done in this session
- Group related changes together
- Keep commits focused and atomic when possible
- NEVER proceed without explicit user approval
- If on `main`, create a feature branch first
- If any step fails, stop and report — don't retry destructive operations

input.command: git status && echo "---" && git diff && echo "---" && git log --oneline -5
input.description: Review git status, diff, and recent log

content.text:
**Plan:**

**1 commit:**
- `apps/www/src/styles/globals.css`
- Message: `fix: override docs sidebar background to match page background in dark mode`

**PR:**
- Title: `fix: docs sidebar dark mode background color`
- Summary: Override `--sidebar` in the www globals so the docs sidebar blends with the page background (`oklch(0.2178 0 0)`) instead of the darker sidebar value introduced in the recent layout refinements merge.

**Merge target:** `main`

Shall I proceed?

content.content: Switched to a new branch 'fix/docs-sidebar-dark-bg'

input.command:
git add apps/www/src/styles/globals.css && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix: override docs sidebar background to match page background in dark mode

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.command:
gh pr create --title "fix: docs sidebar dark mode background color" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Overrides `--sidebar` in `apps/www/src/styles/globals.css` so the docs sidebar background matches the page background (`oklch(0.2178 0 0)`) in dark mode
- The recent layout refinements merge set `--sidebar` to a darker value (`oklch(0.155 0 0)`) in the shared UI theme, causing the docs sidebar to appear visually separated from the page

## Test plan
- [ ] Open docs in dark mode and verify sidebar background matches the page background
EOF
)"

content.text: Let me check how the Sidebar component actually applies its background.

content.content:
30:const SIDEBAR_COOKIE_NAME = "sidebar_state";
75:  // This is the internal state of the sidebar.
88:      // This sets the cookie to keep the sidebar state.
94:  // Helper to toggle the sidebar.
99:  // Adds a keyboard shortcut to toggle the sidebar.
116:  // This makes it easier to style the sidebar with Tailwind classes.
136:          data-slot="sidebar-wrapper"
139:              "--sidebar-width": SIDEBAR_WIDTH,
140:              "--sidebar-width-icon": SIDEBAR_WIDTH_ICON,
141:              "--sidebar-width-header-only": SIDEBAR_WIDTH_HEADER_ONLY,
146:            "group/sidebar-wrapper has-data-[variant=inset]:bg-sidebar flex min-h-svh w-full",
160:  variant = "sidebar",
167:  variant?: "sidebar" | "floating" | "inset";
175:        data-slot="sidebar"
177:          "bg-sidebar text-sidebar-foreground flex h-full w-(--sidebar-width) flex-col",
191:          data-sidebar="sidebar"
192:          data-slot="sidebar"
194:          className="bg-sidebar text-sidebar-foreground w-(--sidebar-width) p-0 [&>button]:hidden"
197:              "--sidebar-width": SIDEBAR_WIDTH_MOBILE,
204:            <SheetDescription>Displays the mobile sidebar.</SheetDescription>
214:      className="group peer text-sidebar-foreground hidden lg:block"
219:      data-slot="sidebar"
221:      {/* This is what handles the sidebar gap on desktop */}
223:        data-slot="sidebar-gap"
225:          "relative w-(--sidebar-width) bg-transparent transition-[width] duration-200 ease-linear",
229:            ? "group-data-[collapsible=icon]:w-[calc(var(--sidebar-width-icon)+(--spacing(4)))]"
230:            : "group-data-[collapsible=icon]:w-(--sidebar-width-icon)",
232:            ? "group-data-[collapsible=header-only]:w-[calc(var(--sidebar-width-header-only)+(--spacing(4)))]"
233:            : "group-data-[collapsible=header-only]:w-(--sidebar-width-header-only)",
237:        data-slot="sidebar-container"

248:            ? "group-data-[collapsible=header-only]:w-[calc(var(--sidebar-width-header-only)+(--spacing(4))+2px)]"
249:            : "group-data-[collapsible=header-only]:w-(--sidebar-width-header-only) group-data-[side=left]:border-r group-data-[side=right]:border-l",

# Oneshot Merge

You are tasked with taking the current changes, committing them, creating a PR, and merging into main — all in one flow with user approval.

## Process:

1. **Understand what changed:**
   - Review the conversation history and understand what was accomplished
   - Run `git status` to see current changes
   - Run `git diff` to understand the modifications
   - Run `git log --oneline -5` to see recent commit history and branch state

2. **Plan your commit(s):**
   - Identify which files belong together
   - Draft clear, descriptive commit messages (imperative mood, focus on why)
   - Consider whether changes should be one commit or multiple logical commits
   - Draft a PR title (under 70 chars) and summary

3. **Present the full plan to the user:**
   - List the files you plan to add for each commit
   - Show the commit message(s) you'll use
   - Show the PR title and summary
   - Confirm: merge target is `main`
   - Ask: "I plan to create [N] commit(s), open a PR, and merge to main. Shall I proceed?"

4. **Execute upon confirmation:**
   - Create a branch if not already on one (use a descriptive branch name)
   - Use `git add` with specific files (never use `-A` or `.`)
   - Create commits with your planned messages
   - Push to remote with `-u` flag
   - Create PR using `gh pr create` with title and body
   - Merge using `gh pr merge --merge --delete-branch`
   - Show the merged PR URL

## Remember:

- You have the full context of what was done in this session
- Group related changes together
- Keep commits focused and atomic when possible
- NEVER proceed without explicit user approval
- If on `main`, create a feature branch first
- If any step fails, stop and report — don't retry destructive operations

content.text:
**Plan:**

**1 commit** across 2 files:
- `apps/www/src/styles/docs.css` — add `--sidebar` override in the right stylesheet
- `apps/www/src/styles/globals.css` — revert the mistaken override from the previous PR

Message: `fix: move docs sidebar bg override to docs.css (correct stylesheet)`

**PR:**
- Title: `fix: docs sidebar dark bg override in correct stylesheet`
- Summary: The previous fix landed in `globals.css` which isn't imported by the docs layout. Moved the `--sidebar` override to `docs.css` (the file actually loaded by the docs route) and reverted the dead code in `globals.css`.

**Merge target:** `main`

Shall I proceed?

content.content: Switched to a new branch 'fix/docs-sidebar-correct-stylesheet'

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.command:
gh pr create --title "fix: docs sidebar dark bg override in correct stylesheet" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Moves `--sidebar` override to `docs.css`, the stylesheet actually imported by the docs layout
- Reverts the dead override from `globals.css` (not loaded by the docs route)

## Root cause
The docs layout at `apps/www/src/app/(app)/(content)/docs/layout.tsx` imports `~/styles/docs.css` directly — `globals.css` is never loaded for that route, so the previous fix had no effect.

## Test plan
- [ ] Open docs in dark mode and verify sidebar background matches the page background
EOF
)"
